đź’ˇNIGHTSPIRE INTEL
==================
đź“ŚInitially found in March 2025
đź“ŚInfected about 120 Victims in short span; since March 2025
đź“ŚAs of now, 34 Countries were targeted excluding CIS and Chinađź‡¨đź‡ł
đź“ŚLike other Ransomware, đź‡şđź‡¸ US tops the Victim List
đź“ŚTaiwanđź‡ąđź‡Ľ, HongKongđź‡­đź‡°, Egyptđź‡Şđź‡¬, Spainđź‡Şđź‡¸ and Turkeyđź‡ąđź‡· are also targeted with multiple infections
đź“ŚThere are 2 DLS found for the group: 1 is a Vanity TOR domain and the other mirror is non-vanity domain
đź“ŚVanity Domain is found to be using PHP/8.2.12 
đź“ŚNon-Vanity TOR Domain is hosted with nginx
đź“Ś.nspire is used to append to encrypte files
đź“ŚAs they have been targeted on a DDoS attack; the group is equipped with Anti-DDoS protection on their DLS
đź“ŚThey are guarded by Cloudflare Service
đź“ŚA sample of Victim's data is displayed in their DLS and also listed them for Sale to Interested Parties
đź“ŚThe group also uses MEGA to store the victim breach
đź“ŚNightspire can be contacted via various medium including: Telegram, Session, TOX, Email
đź“ŚExploited CVE-2024-55591 (as per S-RM Report)
đź“ŚAffiliates of Nightspire found to be worked for Rbfs Ransomware previously as per S-RM Report
đź“ŚA new TOX ID had been added for Affiliate Program in December 2025 to Early 2026
đź“ŚIn January 2026, I found the IP behind NightSpire DLS: 144.172.106.251
đź“ŚThe group started to showcase their succesfull Ransom Negotiation Chat with various clients by masking the identity, who already paid for Decryption Keys
đź“ŚAnalyzing the chat, it is found that the group is gaining access by exploiting vulnerabilities of unpatched services
đź“ŚIn the Data Leak Section, instead of Data Leaks, those are marked as "Selling" for various price range from $100 to $5000

Sample Analysis
===============
đź“ŚAll files are encrypted and adds the extension ".nspire"
đź“ŚCompiled using Go
đź“ŚData Encrypted using: AES via x86 extensions, RC4 PRGA, Salsa20 or ChaCha
đź“ŚData Encode using: Base64, XOR
đź“ŚHashed Message Authentication Code
đź“ŚData Hash using: murmur3, FNV
đź“ŚOn Code Analysis, it is found that the samples of NightSpire has strong co-relation with Snatch Ransomware
